zone_summary: `IsUserError` now classifies HTTP 400 as a user error, explicitly documents that the helper is only for user-facing controller/domain-exception classification (not OneLake/storage 400s), and flips the test matrix expectation for 400 from false to true.

behavioral_changes.summary: `IsUserError` now treats the named enum value `HttpStatusCode.BadRequest` as a user error in the controller-facing allowlist.
`IsUserError` also separately allowlists numeric/casted `400` via `(int)statusCode == 400`, preserving the same classification for raw underlying-value caller shapes.

The method contract is explicitly scoped to user-facing controller/domain-exception classification and explicitly excludes OneLake/storage 400s, which must use `OnelakeBasedFileSystem` classification instead.
The unit-test matrix now locks in HTTP 400 as a user error by flipping the DataRow expectation from `false` to `true`.

grounding_evidence.reason: Contract-bearing XML docs narrow the helper's intended callers and explicitly carve out OneLake/storage-originated 400s from this classifier's domain.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: /// - 400 (BadRequest): Invalid user input/validation errors (e.g., missing displayName)
grounding_evidence.reason: The XML docs add 400/BadRequest to the documented user-error set.
Implementation add: the named `HttpStatusCode.BadRequest` enum value now returns `true` from `IsUserError`.
Implementation add: numeric/casted 400 is explicitly allowlisted as a separate belt-and-suspenders branch.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: [DataRow(400, false)] // BadRequest - Not classified as user error
grounding_evidence.reason: Previous test expectation classified 400 as non-user-error; this is the old side of the assertion flip.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: [DataRow(400, true)] // BadRequest - User error (invalid input, e.g. missing displayName)
grounding_evidence.reason: New test expectation locks 400 into the user-error set; this is the new side of the assertion flip.

scenario_sketches.title: `IsUserError` returns true for `HttpStatusCode.BadRequest`
scenario_sketches.rationale: The allowlist now explicitly includes the named `BadRequest` enum value, so a nominal controller-facing 400 classification should succeed just like the existing 401/403/404/409/422 cases.

scenario_sketches.title: Casted/raw `400` values still classify as user errors
scenario_sketches.rationale: The added `(int)statusCode == 400` arm is independently revertable from the enum arm and should be exercised as a separate caller-shape contract for numeric/casted 400 inputs.

scenario_sketches.title: OneLake/storage-originated 400s remain out of scope for `IsUserError` classification
scenario_sketches.rationale: The new XML docs add a forbidden-caller/domain boundary: controller/domain exceptions may use this helper, but OneLake/storage 400s must be classified elsewhere, so tests should guard against applying the new 400 rule to the wrong error source.

scenario_sketches.title: Regression guard: the IsUserError DataRow matrix expects 400 to be true
scenario_sketches.rationale: The data-driven test row flipped from `400 -> false` to `400 -> true`; preserving that assertion is a distinct CI contract from the implementation change itself.
